summary_table: Grandpa Bud focuses on fortifying the west shore for his livestock. The episode covers the construction of a specialized outbuilding with an X-frame chimney, the strategic digging of a water-filled moat to deter raids, and the successful breeding of the first piglets.
editors_notes: The Brand Rule is applied here by highlighting Grandpa Bud's methodical and calm teaching style. Key strategic moments include his explanation of 'raid-proofing' by managing workbench placement and his troubleshooting of chimney airflow. These segments are high-value for viewers looking for both aesthetic building tips and survival strategies.
ledger_entry: The Norns have seen Conrad turn his gaze to the west shore, where the foundations of a new homestead take root. Grandpa Bud guides us through the quiet labor of taming the wild boars and the rhythmic swing of the pickaxe as he carves a moat from the earth. There is a wisdom in his pace—building not just for today, but for the coming Silver Age. We witness the birth of the first piglets, a sign of prosperity, and the careful planting of oak and birch to shade the future. It is a tale of fortification and foresight, ensuring the hearth stays lit and the livestock stay safe from the shadows of the Black Forest.

type_a_shorts.description: The X-Frame Chimney: A stylish and functional build tip for better airflow and aesthetics.
Moat Strategy: How to dig down to sea level to create a permanent water barrier against raids.
The First Piglets: A heartwarming success moment showing the results of patient boar taming.

type_b_clips.description: Building the Outbuilding: A narrative look at Grandpa Bud's architectural process, focusing on the roof and chimney details.
Strategic Fortification: Grandpa Bud explains the rationale behind the trench placement to protect the farm and livestock.
Roadmap to the Silver Age: A strategic wrap-up detailing the transition from iron to silver and future smelting plans.

type_c_arc.description: The Green Thumb Montage: A peaceful sequence of planting oak and birch saplings to create a sustainable forest.
The Great Trench: A focused montage of the labor-intensive process of digging the perimeter moat.
